title: Lovely Juicy Moroccan Garlic Mechoui
description: A flavorful and aromatic Moroccan dish featuring a slow-cooked lamb shoulder with a rich garlic sauce, perfect for a weekend brunch.

ingredients.name: lamb shoulder
ingredients.notes: look for a bone-in shoulder for better flavor

instructions: Season the lamb shoulder with salt, black pepper, and cumin.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Brown the lamb shoulder on all sides, about 5 minutes per side.
Remove the lamb from the pot and set it aside.
Reduce the heat to medium and cook the chopped onion until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic, grated ginger, ground coriander, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper to the pot.
Add the browned lamb shoulder back to the pot, along with the lemon juice and honey.
Cover the pot with a lid and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Slow cook for 2-3 hours, or until the lamb is tender and falls apart easily.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh parsley and crusty bread on the side.
